architecture.summary: A post-training refinement framework that operates on latent reasoning embeddings produced by models like Coconut. Information flows through three stages: (1) a latent reasoning backbone (Coconut) that iteratively updates an embedding via fixed model blocks without generating tokens; (2) contrastive reasoning feedback search that compares embeddings from weak and strong reference models to compute a gradient signal guiding the embedding toward better reasoning directions; (3) residual embedding refinement that blends current and previous embeddings via fixed-weight interpolation to stabilize multi-step updates. Both refinement modules operate in the latent space using forward passes only, with no parameter updates.

biblio.title: Efficient Post-Training Refinement of Latent Reasoning in Large Language Models

claims.statement: +5% accuracy gain on MathQA without additional training
On AQUA-RAT, achieves +2.76% absolute gain over Coconut
On ProsQA, achieves +3.67% absolute gain over Coconut
Reduces token generation by over 92% compared to Chain-of-Thought on MathQA and AQUA
Inference-only method achieves +4.47% accuracy on ProsQA with 24 seconds and 31.23GB memory, while training+inference approach takes 54+ minutes and 39.04GB but yields only +1.63% gain
Ablation: residual refinement alone yields +4.63% on MathQA; contrastive search alone yields +4.03%; both together yield +5.10%
Method improves accuracy over Coconut baseline on all three model architectures tested (GPT-2 117M, Qwen-2.5 1.5B, LLaMA-3.2 3B) with gains ranging from +1.77% to +5.10% on MathQA

On GSM8K, Chain-of-Thought remains most effective (42.76% accuracy) as GSM8K contains complex arithmetic problems requiring symbolic calculations that latent reasoning struggles with without external tools
